label: Algebra
shortDescription: Equations, functions, polynomials, exponents, and the language of symbolic mathematics.
modulesDescription: Read the algebra lessons in order, or jump directly to the topic you need.
ogDescription: Master algebra foundations, equations and inequalities, functions and graphs, polynomials and factoring, exponents radicals and logarithms, plus complex numbers and sequences — with detailed explanations, worked examples, common mistakes, and practice questions.
courseDescription: A complete introductory algebra course covering the language of algebra (variables, expressions, equations, inequalities, order of operations), solving linear quadratic and systems of equations plus inequalities, functions their domains ranges graphs transformations inverses, polynomials monomials binomials factoring division and rational expressions, exponents radicals rational exponents exponential functions and logarithms, and advanced topics including complex numbers, quadratic formula, discriminant, and arithmetic/geometric sequences. Features ELI5 intuition, step-by-step worked examples, common pitfalls, and hundreds of practice problems.

topics.title: Algebra
topics.description: The branch of mathematics that uses symbols and letters to represent numbers and quantities in formulas and equations.

topics.title: Variable (mathematics)
topics.description: A symbol, usually a letter, that represents an unknown or changeable number in mathematical expressions and equations.

topics.title: Expression (mathematics)
topics.description: A finite combination of symbols and numbers that represents a value, using operations but without an equals sign.

topics.title: Equation
topics.description: A mathematical statement that asserts the equality of two expressions, typically solved to find the value of the variable.

topics.title: Inequality (mathematics)
topics.description: A mathematical statement that compares two expressions using symbols like <, >, ≤, or ≥ rather than equality.

topics.title: Order of operations
topics.description: The agreed-upon sequence (PEMDAS/BODMAS) for evaluating mathematical expressions with multiple operations.

topics.title: Linear equation
topics.description: An equation that can be written in the form ax + b = 0 where the variable appears to the first power only.

topics.title: Quadratic equation
topics.description: An equation of the form ax² + bx + c = 0 that can have up to two real solutions.

topics.title: System of linear equations
topics.description: A set of two or more linear equations in the same variables whose common solutions satisfy all equations simultaneously.

topics.title: Absolute value
topics.description: The distance of a number from zero on the number line, always non-negative and denoted |x|.

topics.title: Inequality
topics.description: A statement that one expression is greater than, less than, or not equal to another, solved similarly to equations but with care for inequality direction.

topics.title: Absolute value equation
topics.description: An equation involving the absolute value of an expression, typically solved by considering cases or using properties of | |.

topics.title: Absolute value inequality
topics.description: An inequality involving absolute value, solved by rewriting as compound inequalities based on the definition of absolute value.

topics.title: Quadratic inequality
topics.description: An inequality involving a quadratic expression, solved by finding roots and testing intervals on a sign chart.

topics.title: Rational equation
topics.description: An equation containing one or more rational expressions (fractions with polynomial numerators and denominators).

topics.title: Function (mathematics)
topics.description: A relation that assigns to each element in the domain exactly one element in the codomain or range.

topics.title: Domain of a function
topics.description: The complete set of possible input values for which the function produces a defined real output.

topics.title: Range (mathematics)
topics.description: The set of all possible output values a function can produce as the input varies over its domain.

topics.title: Graph of a function
topics.description: The visual representation of all ordered pairs (x, f(x)) plotted on the coordinate plane.

topics.title: Linear function
topics.description: A function whose graph is a straight line, of the form f(x) = mx + b with constant slope m.

topics.title: Quadratic function
topics.description: A function of the form f(x) = ax² + bx + c whose graph is a parabola opening up or down.

topics.title: Piecewise function
topics.description: A function defined by different formulas or rules on different intervals of its domain.

topics.title: Transformation of functions
topics.description: Operations that shift, stretch, compress, or reflect the graph of a function to produce a new related function.

topics.title: Even and odd functions
topics.description: Functions with symmetry: even functions satisfy f(-x) = f(x) (y-axis symmetry) and odd satisfy f(-x) = -f(x) (origin symmetry).

topics.title: Inverse function
topics.description: A function that reverses the action of the original function, existing when the original is one-to-one (bijective onto its range).

topics.title: Polynomial
topics.description: An expression consisting of variables and coefficients combined using only addition, subtraction, and multiplication (non-negative integer powers).

topics.title: Monomial
topics.description: A polynomial consisting of a single term: a product of a coefficient and variables raised to non-negative integer powers.

topics.title: Binomial (polynomial)
topics.description: A polynomial that is the sum or difference of exactly two monomial terms.

topics.title: Factorization
topics.description: The process of rewriting a polynomial or number as a product of simpler factors or polynomials.

topics.title: Difference of two squares
topics.description: A special factorization pattern a² − b² = (a − b)(a + b) useful for simplifying and solving.

topics.title: Sum and difference of cubes
topics.description: Factorization identities a³ + b³ = (a + b)(a² − ab + b²) and a³ − b³ = (a − b)(a² + ab + b²).

topics.title: Polynomial long division
topics.description: An algorithm for dividing one polynomial by another to obtain a quotient and remainder, analogous to numeric long division.

topics.title: Synthetic division
topics.description: A shortcut method for dividing a polynomial by a linear factor of the form (x − c), using only coefficients.

topics.title: Rational expression
topics.description: A quotient of two polynomials, also called a rational function when viewed as f(x) = p(x)/q(x).

topics.title: Exponentiation
topics.description: The operation of raising a base to a power, written a^n, representing repeated multiplication of the base.

topics.title: Power rule
topics.description: The exponent rule (a^m)^n = a^(m n) and a^m * a^n = a^(m+n) for simplifying expressions with powers.

topics.title: Square root
topics.description: The inverse operation of squaring: √x is the non-negative number whose square is x.

topics.title: nth root
topics.description: The number whose nth power equals the radicand, generalizing square roots to cube roots, fourth roots, etc.

topics.title: Rational exponent
topics.description: An exponent that is a fraction p/q, equivalent to taking the qth root and then raising to the p power.

topics.title: Exponential function
topics.description: A function of the form f(x) = a * b^x where the variable appears in the exponent; models growth and decay.

topics.title: Logarithm
topics.description: The inverse of exponentiation: log_b(a) is the exponent to which b must be raised to obtain a.

topics.title: Radical equation
topics.description: An equation in which the variable appears inside a root (radical) symbol, typically solved by isolating the radical and raising to a power.

topics.title: Complex number
topics.description: A number of the form a + bi where i² = −1, extending the reals with an imaginary part for solving all polynomials.

topics.title: Imaginary unit
topics.description: The number i defined by i² = −1, the foundation of the complex number system.

topics.title: Quadratic formula
topics.description: The formula x = [−b ± √(b² − 4ac)] / (2a) that gives the solutions to any quadratic equation ax² + bx + c = 0.

topics.title: Discriminant
topics.description: The expression b² − 4ac under the radical in the quadratic formula; its sign tells the number and type of roots.

topics.title: Arithmetic sequence
topics.description: A sequence in which each term after the first is obtained by adding a constant difference d to the preceding term.

topics.title: Geometric sequence
topics.description: A sequence in which each term after the first is obtained by multiplying the preceding term by a constant ratio r.
